title: logformatter: update MAGIC BLOB string
body:
Fallout from libpod->podman repo name move: the HTML logs
created by logformatter are no longer accessible. They
render as:

    https://storage.googleapis.com/SECRET-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6313596734930944/html/integration_test.log.html

(yes, "SECRET" instead of "cirrus-ci". I have no idea what
that's about, but that's not the focus of this PR, and I'm
going to ignore it for the time being and hope it goes away).

The focus of this PR is to change "podman" in the URL above
back to "libpod", because that's what the Google storage
bucket is called. This is unfortunate hardcoding, because
the way the code was written it used $CIRRUS_REPO_FULL_NAME
as defined by Cirrus.

I have no idea if this will work. It's possible that instead
of this, the proper change is to update the magic blob string.
Chris is OOTO this week, so I'll just try random fixes in
hopes of getting something to work.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Well, aside from the fact that it had no effect, but thanks.

It looks like I may have to change the "magic blob" string. The source of that is [this comment by cevich](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/5344#issuecomment-592680801) on #5344, in particular:

> IIRC, the `STATIC_MAGIC_BLOB` is formed from some combination of an internal github ID and the google project number.  I/We could do more research to expose the magic better if needed.

I've looked everywhere I can think of, and cannot find any documentation on how this magic string is generated nor what its new value may be. I've looked in the source tree, in github history, IRC logs, mail logs, and even the googleapi web UI.

I'm afraid this may be unsolvable until @cevich returns.

/hold

@cevich  I need help again please. Something somewhere is filtering `cirrus-ci` and replacing it with `SECRET`:
```
https://storage.googleapis.com/SECRET-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5595074811658240/html/integration_test.log.html
```
(Source: [sample cirrus log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5595074811658240?command=integration_test#L8354), happening in both integration and system test logs.)

If I copy that URL, manually change `SECRET` to `cirrus-ci`, request fails with:
```
AccessDeniedAccess denied.Anonymous caller does not have storage.objects.get access to the Google Cloud Storage object.
```
The latter could maybe be related to the `Public access` setting [here](https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser?project=libpod-218412): the new blob says "Subject to object ACLs", whereas the old bucket said "Public to internet". If you agree that this is the difference, could you make the magic necessary change please?

But then that still leaves us with the SECRET thing. Will that be fixed if the ACL issue is fixed? Or is it something else?

Yes I think you're right about the access problem, I have an idea the other thing is related to my copy-paste encrypting all our "secret" values (due to project rename).  I believe one of them is literally "cirruc-ci" and that's why it's being masked...

...https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/.cirrus.yml#L74 try that one, kill the encrypted string and replace with 'cirruc-ci'.  That will either break horribly or fix the `SECRET` problem :grin:   I'll see about fixing the bucket permissions right now...
Thank you! PR updated and pushed.

...okay, access is fixed and I disabled the old bucket, made it read-only.  There's no way to change the bucket name or add a description, otherwise I would have done that as well.  Okay now, where to document these arcane incantations.

WHEW! Cirrus logs now display working links at the bottom. (Sample [integration](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5110197549006848/html/integration_test.log.html), [system](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5110197549006848/html/system_test.log.html)). Thank you @cevich for your help. This was a double whammy(with the coincident SECRET problem) and I couldn't possibly have gotten anywhere without your experience.

/hold cancel

Happy to help, and wishing I could have documented some of these things better...while putting out fires from the renaming...oof :confounded: In hindsight, at least things turned out to be failing a logical way (for me).  I'll those any day, over unpredictable/random type problems :grinning:

From my perspective, this is ready to merge. To fully confirm that it works, click on any of the CI results that include system or integration tests, then expand that log, go to the bottom, and confirm (copy-paste unfortunately) that the formatted-results log works and displays the correct, readable log results.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
